channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The EU beat COVID and ditched Russian energy. Now it must learn to live without US security help
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Over the last five years, European Union countries have been forced to adapt to unprecedented circumstances. They pulled together to purchase tens of millions of vaccines and devised an innovative debt financing scheme to resuscitate their COVID-19-ravaged economies.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pope continues slow recovery from double pneumonia
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What you need to know about Adnan Syed's murder conviction
item.description: The legal battle over Adnan Syed 's conviction, scrutinized a decade ago in the hit podcast “Serial,” keeps twisting and turning, even after prosecutors freed him from more than 23 years in prison for a murder he still says he didn't commit.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected serial killer's 1980s summer job may have been road map to murders: prosecutors
item.description: Suspected Gilgo serial killer Rex Heuermann had a summer job that police say primed him with knowledge about his alleged chosen dump site for victims' remains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meet the only 2 passengers from the viral 'private' Southwest flight out of Utah
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- It's not every day that a commercial airplane is transformed into a "private" plane -- but two Utah students got to see what it's like when that does happen.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Nurses' stories recount terror of armed man's attack at Pennsylvania hospital
item.description: The stories of two nurses who survived a gunman's attack on an intensive care unit in a York, Pennsylvania, hospital are being shared in Facebook posts that reveal new details.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Utah is one step away from becoming 1st state to ban fluoride in water
item.description: The Utah governor's signature is all that stands between a state ban on water fluoridation after House Bill 81 cleared its final hurdle in the legislature on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Drachman, WGN 9

item.title: A Texas child who was not vaccinated has died of measles, a first for the US in a decade
item.description: LUBBOCK, Texas (AP) — A child who wasn't vaccinated died in a measles outbreak in rural West Texas, state officials said Wednesday, the first U.S. death from the highly contagious — but preventable — respiratory disease since 2015.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Texas lottery drawings that paid out big jackpots are the focus of widening investigatons
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Two major lottery drawings in Texas that put nearly $180 million in the pockets of winning ticket holders have set off widening state investigations over concern that ticket sellers and buyers may have exploited the rules. The Texas Lottery, one of the largest in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department abandoning cases alleging discriminatory police and firefighter hiring
item.description: The Justice Department is abandoning Biden administration cases that sought to force police and fire departments to end alleged discriminatory hiring practices, Attorney General Pam Bondi said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Carolina man set to face a firing squad seeks postponement, questioning execution procedures
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who is scheduled to die next week by firing squad is again asking that his execution be postponed and prison officials release more information about the state's lethal injection drug and procedures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Bodycam video shows arrest of 4 suspects in FedEx driver robbery
item.description: In the bodycam video, two are seen being apprehended by police, before an officer chases down and handcuffs the final suspect shortly after.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, NewsNation

item.title: Man assaulted by as many as 30 teenage bicyclists in Los Angeles, police say
item.description: A man was brutally attacked in the Carthay neighborhood of Los Angeles, California, by a large group of teenage bicyclists, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bell tolls as New York marks the anniversary of the 1993 World Trade Center bombing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City on Wednesday marked the anniversary of the 1993 bombing at the old World Trade Center, when Islamic extremists first attempted to bring down the twin towers with 1,200 pounds of explosives in a parked rental van.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MINCHILLO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 3 councilmen in South Carolina's third-largest city charged in bribery and kickback scheme
item.description: Three members of the City Council in South Carolina's third-largest city accepted kickbacks and bribes to give out grant money and rezone land or lied about what they knew, federal prosecutors said Wednesday, Charges against North Charleston councilmen Jerome Heyward, Sandino Moses and Mike A.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump: ‘Concession Agreement’ with Venezuela will be terminated
item.description: In his post, Trump slammed the “Electoral conditions” within Venezuela and argued Maduro’s regime failed to meet the concessions given by Biden.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge weighs continued freedom for Adnan Syed in 'Serial' murder case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — More than a decade after Adnan Syed’s murder case attracted massive interest through the hit podcast “Serial,” a Baltimore judge is considering a request to reduce his sentence to time served, which would allow him to remain free indefinitely even though his conviction still stands.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: After a month of Trump's pro-oil and gas moves, Dems target his energy emergency
item.description: President Donald Trump pledged a U-turn away from the Biden administration's commitment to fighting climate change and promoting clean energy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Phillis And Jennifer Mcdermott, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Agriculture secretary outlines plan to lower egg prices
item.description: “The Agriculture Department will invest up to $1 billion to curb this crisis and make eggs affordable again," Rollins wrote in an op-ed.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Agriculture secretary outlines plan to lower egg prices
item.description: “The Agriculture Department will invest up to $1 billion to curb this crisis and make eggs affordable again," Rollins wrote.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Child becomes first measles death reported in Texas outbreak
item.description: An unvaccinated school-aged child who was hospitalized with measles has passed away, marking the first death in the outbreak, according to Lubbock and state health officials.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Rooney, Fox 5

item.title: USAID employees given 15 minutes to clear out offices
item.description: U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees were notified late Tuesday they would be given 15 minutes to collect their belongings from their offices as the Trump administration forges ahead with gutting the agency.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. targeting childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Childhood psychiatrists insist the drugs, for attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and depression, are nonaddictive and proven safe and say they are more concerned about young Americans unable to access psychiatric medications that could help.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KTLA 5

item.title: Things to know about the ruling blocking President Trump's refugee ban
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump's effort to suspend the system for resettling refugees in the U.S. is on hold after a federal judge in Seattle blocked it. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge OKs prison abuse settlement, rejecting Trump administration's push to rewrite protections
item.description: A judge on Tuesday gave final approval to a legal settlement that subjects the federal Bureau of Prisons to independent monitoring and increased transparency in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse at a now-shuttered federal women’s prison in California. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A federal judge in Seattle blocks Trump’s effort to halt the refugee admissions system
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge in Seattle on Tuesday blocked President Donald Trump’s suspension of the nation’s refugee admissions system, saying that while the president has broad authority over who comes into the country, he cannot nullify the law passed by Congress establishing the program.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rare video captures super pod of 2,000 dolphins breaching and playing
item.description: A California boat captain has captured rare video of a super pod of more than 2,000 dolphins breaching off the coast of Monterey Bay, including the more elusive northern right whale dolphin.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Scenes at US Capitol on war's anniversary reflect partisan divide on Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 100 House members at one point packed the steps of the U.S. Capitol to show their support for Ukraine following Russia's invasion three years ago, Republicans joining with Democrats in a forceful display of bipartisanship.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump suspends security clearances for Jack Smith's outside lawyers
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed a memo suspending security clearances for a number of outside lawyers who are defending former special counsel Jack Smith in his personal capacity. White House staff secretary Will Scharf said in the Oval Office that the memo targeted Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Missouri woman accused in plot to sell Graceland says she'll plead guilty
item.description: A Missouri woman said Tuesday she is pleading guilty to a federal charge accusing her of concocting a brazen plot to defraud Elvis Presley’s family by trying to auction off his Graceland mansion and property before a judge halted the mysterious foreclosure sale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Man steals 2 bulldog puppies as another 'fakes seizure' to distract staff
item.description: Two bulldog puppies were taken from a pet store in Colorado after a man "allegedly faked a seizure to distract employees," the Arapahoe Sheriff's Office said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why you won't receive a Supplemental Security Income check in March
item.description: It's the latest blip in the payment schedule but don't worry — beneficiaries will still receive all of their payments.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, Fox 5
